page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server instance featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 144 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture includes 504 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 2232 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 7.75 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The server achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, PassMark CPU testing, and software compilation. Memory subsystem performance is highly rated, with cached read bandwidth reaching 23104.88 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth at 412.54 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a Redis SET throughput of 423.02 million operations per second and top-tier PassMark database scores. However, image processing falls into the average tier, and LLM text generation for small 135M models registers in the bottom 25% tier.

From a resource density perspective, this bare-metal configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and highly parallelized workloads by consolidating massive compute and memory footprints onto a single physical node. The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which introduces a performance tradeoff for I/O-bound applications. The lack of hardware accelerators limits its utility for GPU-dependent deep learning, though the built-in Intel AMX instructions mitigate this for certain CPU-based matrix operations.

This server is designed for large-scale enterprise databases, in-memory data grids, heavy software compilation pipelines, and high-throughput web serving. It is also suitable for CPU-based machine learning inference and complex physical simulations that benefit from high memory bandwidth and massive parallel thread execution.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 288 dedicated vCPUs across 144 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 7.75 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis throughput reaching 423.02 million operations per second. However, image processing and small-model LLM text generation show average to weak performance. This instance provides high resource density for memory-bound and highly parallelized workloads, making it a strong fit for large-scale in-memory databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, though users must rely on network storage for I/O requirements.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2232 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 dedicated vCPUs and 2232 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with 288 dedicated vCPUs for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors at 4.2 GHz.
2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory providing 7.75 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Optimized for in-memory databases, software compilation, and parallel computing.
